title: European Union — QARC Project Execution
content:
Country: European Union
Organization: European Union Horizon Europe Program
Type: Phase | Phase: Research
Period: 2026–2028
Title: QARC Project Execution
Description: Brno University of Technology coordinates EU Horizon QARC (Quantum-Resistant Cryptography in Practice) project focusing on hybrid schemes cryptographic agility and real-world PQC deployment pilots across EU eGovernment and cloud infrastructures.

title: Global — Quantum Safe Explorer Launched
content:
Country: Global
Organization: International Business Machines Corporation
Type: Milestone | Phase: POC
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Quantum Safe Explorer Launched
Description: IBM launches Quantum Safe Explorer tool to help organizations scan source and object code to identify cryptographic assets dependencies and vulnerabilities creating a CBOM.

title: Global — IBM Heron R2 Processor Available
content:
Country: Global
Organization: International Business Machines Corporation
Type: Milestone | Phase: POC
Period: 2025–2025
Title: IBM Heron R2 Processor Available
Description: IBM makes Heron R2 processor available with 156 qubits designed to enable more complex quantum circuits with low error rates facilitating quantum utility at scale.

title: Global — IBM Next-Gen Processor Development
content:
Country: Global
Organization: International Business Machines Corporation
Type: Milestone | Phase: Research
Period: 2025–2025
Title: IBM Next-Gen Processor Development
Description: IBM advances development of its next-generation modular quantum processor incorporating features for high-efficiency quantum error correction targeting early 2026 testing milestones.

title: Global — IBM Kookaburra Processor Released
content:
Country: Global
Organization: International Business Machines Corporation
Type: Milestone | Phase: POC
Period: 2026–2026
Title: IBM Kookaburra Processor Released
Description: IBM releases Kookaburra first modular 1386-qubit processor designed to store and process encoded information combining quantum memory with logic operations.

title: Global — IBM Quantum Advantage Target
content:
Country: Global
Organization: International Business Machines Corporation
Type: Milestone | Phase: Research
Period: 2026–2026
Title: IBM Quantum Advantage Target
Description: IBM targets achieving and verifying quantum advantage demonstrating verifiable speed-ups over classical computing for meaningful tasks.

title: Global — Quantum Safe Program Launch
content:
Country: Global
Organization: Microsoft Corporation
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2023–2023
Title: Quantum Safe Program Launch
Description: Microsoft formally launches Quantum Safe Program (QSP) to build quantum-safe future across all products and services.

title: Global — Majorana 1 Quantum Chip
content:
Country: Global
Organization: Microsoft Corporation
Type: Milestone | Phase: Research
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Majorana 1 Quantum Chip
Description: Microsoft announces development of Majorana 1 quantum chip.

title: Global — Microsoft QSP Roadmap Published
content:
Country: Global
Organization: Microsoft Corporation
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Microsoft QSP Roadmap Published
Description: Microsoft publishes comprehensive roadmap detailing progress toward next-generation cryptography and 2033 completion target.

title: Global — Core Infrastructure Transition
content:
Country: Global
Organization: Microsoft Corporation
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2026–2027
Title: Core Infrastructure Transition
Description: Microsoft accelerates QSP transition for core infrastructure including signing services and network services starting in 2026 with Windows Azure M365 integration beginning 2027.

title: Global — Early Quantum-Safe Adoption
content:
Country: Global
Organization: Microsoft Corporation
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2029–2029
Title: Early Quantum-Safe Adoption
Description: Microsoft begins early adoption of quantum-safe capabilities across products and services with quantum-safe systems gradually becoming default.

title: Global — Microsoft Full PQC Transition
content:
Country: Global
Organization: Microsoft Corporation
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2033–2033
Title: Microsoft Full PQC Transition
Description: Microsoft completes full transition to quantum-ready cryptographic standards across all products and services. Two years ahead of 2035 government deadlines.

title: Global — UltraPQ-Suite Launched
content:
Country: Global
Organization: PQShield Ltd
Type: Milestone | Phase: POC
Period: 2025–2025
Title: UltraPQ-Suite Launched
Description: PQShield launches UltraPQ-Suite collection of specialized PQC implementations including PQPlatform-TrustSys quantum-safe Root of Trust for ASIC/FPGA CNSA 2.0 compliance.

title: Global — 100th Research Paper Milestone
content:
Country: Global
Organization: PQShield Ltd
Type: Milestone | Phase: Research
Period: 2025–2025
Title: 100th Research Paper Milestone
Description: PQShield releases 100th research paper achieving 24 top-tier conference papers in 2025 including Distinguished Paper Award at CCS.

title: Global — Carahsoft Government Partnership
content:
Country: Global
Organization: PQShield Ltd
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Carahsoft Government Partnership
Description: PQShield partners with Carahsoft to make PQC solutions accessible to US government agencies.

title: Global — Post-Quantum VPN Research Initiated
content:
Country: Global
Organization: PQShield Ltd
Type: Milestone | Phase: Research
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Post-Quantum VPN Research Initiated
Description: PQShield initiates new research into Post-Quantum VPNs and expands formal verification efforts for real-world FHE operations.

title: United States — AQtive Guard Pentagon 5-Year Agreement
content:
Country: United States
Organization: SandboxAQ Inc
Type: Milestone | Phase: POC
Period: 2025–2025
Title: AQtive Guard Pentagon 5-Year Agreement
Description: SandboxAQ secures five-year agreement with Department of Defense CIO (formerly referred to as Department of War CIO per late-2025 rebranding) to deploy AQtive Guard cybersecurity platform across Pentagon systems for automated cryptographic discovery inventory and managed PQC transition. Paves way for other DoD agencies.

title: United States — AQtive Guard FedRAMP Ready
content:
Country: United States
Organization: SandboxAQ Inc
Type: Milestone | Phase: Regulation
Period: 2025–2025
Title: AQtive Guard FedRAMP Ready
Description: SandboxAQ achieves FedRAMP Ready status for AQtive Guard platform providing standardized path for federal agencies to evaluate automated cryptographic discovery.

title: United States — RWPQC 2025 Conference
content:
Country: United States
Organization: SandboxAQ MITRE and Linux Foundation PQCA
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: RWPQC 2025 Conference
Description: SandboxAQ MITRE and Linux Foundation PQCA co-host Real World Post Quantum Cryptography (RWPQC) 2025 conference.

title: Global — Quantum-Safe Secure Element Pilot
content:
Country: Global
Organization: NXP Semiconductors with PQShield
Type: Milestone | Phase: POC
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Quantum-Safe Secure Element Pilot
Description: NXP begins sampling secure-element pilot incorporating PQShield quantum-safe core in Q3 2025.

title: Australia — Initial PQC Planning Guide
content:
Country: Australia
Organization: Australian Signals Directorate
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2022–2022
Title: Initial PQC Planning Guide
Description: ASD publishes initial Planning for Post-Quantum Cryptography guidance.

title: Australia — ISM December 2024 Update
content:
Country: Australia
Organization: Australian Signals Directorate
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2024–2024
Title: ISM December 2024 Update
Description: ASD updates ISM with specific PQC requirements (ISM-1917) including ML-DSA-87, ML-KEM-1024, SHA-384/512, AES-256 by 2030.

title: Australia — Migration Planning Phase
content:
Country: Australia
Organization: Australian Signals Directorate
Type: Phase | Phase: Discovery
Period: 2024–2026
Title: Migration Planning Phase
Description: Organizations develop refined PQC transition plan accounting for security goals, risk tolerances, and data value.

title: Australia — PQC Planning Guide Updated
content:
Country: Australia
Organization: Australian Signals Directorate
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: PQC Planning Guide Updated
Description: ASD publishes updated Planning for PQC guidance with LATICE framework and detailed milestones.

title: Australia — Critical Systems Migration Start
content:
Country: Australia
Organization: Australian Signals Directorate
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2026–2028
Title: Critical Systems Migration Start
Description: Implementation of PQC algorithms begins with most critical and sensitive systems.

title: Australia — Full Migration Phase
content:
Country: Australia
Organization: Australian Signals Directorate
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2028–2030
Title: Full Migration Phase
Description: Complete transition to ASD-approved PQC algorithms across all systems.

title: Australia — Traditional Crypto Prohibited
content:
Country: Australia
Organization: Australian Signals Directorate
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2030–2030
Title: Traditional Crypto Prohibited
Description: Cease use of traditional asymmetric cryptography (RSA, ECDSA, ECDH). Full transition to PQC. 5 years ahead of US/UK.

title: International — Paper 158 Quantum Roadmap
content:
Country: International
Organization: Bank for International Settlements
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Paper 158 Quantum Roadmap
Description: BIS publishes Paper 158: Quantum-Readiness Roadmap for financial systems. Critical systems quantum-safe by early 2030s.

title: Canada — PQC Best Practices Published
content:
Country: Canada
Organization: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2023–2023
Title: PQC Best Practices Published
Description: CFDIR publishes Canadian National Quantum-Readiness Best Practices and Guidelines.

title: Canada — PQC Migration Roadmap Published
content:
Country: Canada
Organization: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: PQC Migration Roadmap Published
Description: CCCS publishes ITSM.40.001 Roadmap for migration to PQC for Government of Canada. Effective June 23 2025.

title: Canada — SPIN Notice Published
content:
Country: Canada
Organization: Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2025–2025
Title: SPIN Notice Published
Description: Security Policy Implementation Notice on PQC migration effective October 9, 2025 under Policy on Government Security.

title: Canada — Departmental Planning Phase
content:
Country: Canada
Organization: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security
Type: Phase | Phase: Discovery
Period: 2025–2026
Title: Departmental Planning Phase
Description: Federal departments develop initial departmental PQC migration plans. Due by April 2026.

title: Canada — Departmental PQC Migration Plans Due
content:
Country: Canada
Organization: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Departmental PQC Migration Plans Due
Description: Federal departments must develop and submit initial PQC migration plans to CCCS by April 2026 with annual progress reporting thereafter. New IT procurements should include PQC vendor support clauses as an ongoing obligation under the roadmap.

title: Canada — High Priority Migration Phase
content:
Country: Canada
Organization: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2026–2031
Title: High Priority Migration Phase
Description: Migration of high priority government systems. Annual progress reporting required.

title: Canada — High Priority Systems Complete
content:
Country: Canada
Organization: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2031–2031
Title: High Priority Systems Complete
Description: Completion of PQC migration for high priority government systems.

title: Canada — Remaining Systems Migration
content:
Country: Canada
Organization: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2031–2035
Title: Remaining Systems Migration
Description: Migration of all remaining non-classified government systems.

title: Canada — Full GC Migration Complete
content:
Country: Canada
Organization: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2035–2035
Title: Full GC Migration Complete
Description: Completion of PQC migration for all Government of Canada non-classified systems.

title: China — CACR PQC Competition Results
content:
Country: China
Organization: Chinese Association for Cryptologic Research
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2020–2020
Title: CACR PQC Competition Results
Description: CACR announces results of its post-quantum cryptography competition selecting initial algorithms (36 submissions).

title: China — NGCC Program Launched
content:
Country: China
Organization: Institute of Commercial Cryptography Standards
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2025–2025
Title: NGCC Program Launched
Description: China launches Next-Generation Commercial Cryptographic (NGCC) Algorithms Program for PQC standardization. Source article published February 18, 2025; program launch date per cited article.

title: China — Hybrid Quantum-Safe System Launched
content:
Country: China
Organization: China Telecom Quantum Group
Type: Milestone | Phase: Migration
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Hybrid Quantum-Safe System Launched
Description: China Telecom launches world's first distributed cryptography system combining QKD and PQC. Demonstrates quantum-encrypted communications across a 1,000km+ multi-city network spanning 16 cities (not the direct Beijing-Hefei distance of ~370km). Network includes Beijing-Hefei link.

title: China — NGCC Algorithm Evaluation
content:
Country: China
Organization: Cyberspace Administration of China
Type: Phase | Phase: Research
Period: 2025–2027
Title: NGCC Algorithm Evaluation
Description: NGCC program evaluates submitted algorithms for security, performance, and implementation feasibility.

title: Czech Republic — Cryptographic Recommendations
content:
Country: Czech Republic
Organization: National Cyber and Information Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2023–2023
Title: Cryptographic Recommendations
Description: NUKIB publishes Minimum Requirements for cryptographic algorithms with PQC guidance.

title: Czech Republic — Key Establishment Migration
content:
Country: Czech Republic
Organization: National Cyber and Information Security Agency
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2024–2027
Title: Key Establishment Migration
Description: Migration for key establishment and encryption systems.

title: Czech Republic — Encryption Migration Complete
content:
Country: Czech Republic
Organization: National Cyber and Information Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2027–2027
Title: Encryption Migration Complete
Description: Complete migration by 2027 for key establishment and encryption. Firmware/software signing as soon as possible.

title: European Union — PQC Integration Study
content:
Country: European Union
Organization: European Union Agency for Cybersecurity
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2022–2022
Title: PQC Integration Study
Description: ENISA publishes Post-Quantum Cryptography: Integration Study examining technology landscape.

title: European Union — EU PQC Recommendation Published
content:
Country: European Union
Organization: European Commission
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2024–2024
Title: EU PQC Recommendation Published
Description: European Commission publishes Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101 on Coordinated Implementation Roadmap for PQC transition.

title: European Union — Critical Infrastructure Migration
content:
Country: European Union
Organization: European Commission
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2024–2030
Title: Critical Infrastructure Migration
Description: Migration of critical infrastructure and high-risk systems to quantum-safe cryptography.

title: European Union — Coordinated Roadmap v1.1
content:
Country: European Union
Organization: NIS Cooperation Group
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Coordinated Roadmap v1.1
Description: NIS Cooperation Group publishes Coordinated Implementation Roadmap for PQC Transition Version 1.1.

title: European Union — QSFF Call to Action
content:
Country: European Union
Organization: European Union Agency for Law Enforcement Cooperation
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: QSFF Call to Action
Description: Europol Quantum Safe Financial Forum publishes call to action. Notes 86% of executives unprepared.

title: European Union — EU PQC Survey Launched
content:
Country: European Union
Organization: NIS Cooperation Group
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2025–2025
Title: EU PQC Survey Launched
Description: NIS Cooperation Group PQC Work Stream launches public survey to gather feedback on Coordinated Implementation Roadmap. Feedback deadline September 29, 2025.

title: European Union — Agreed Cryptographic Algorithms v2
content:
Country: European Union
Organization: European Cybersecurity Certification Group
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Agreed Cryptographic Algorithms v2
Description: ECCG publishes Agreed Cryptographic Algorithms version 2 (May 2025) with PQC guidance. Recommends PQ/T hybrid schemes and adjusted symmetric key lengths.

title: European Union — Member State Strategy Initiation
content:
Country: European Union
Organization: European Commission
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Member State Strategy Initiation
Description: All EU Member States should initiate national PQC transition strategies by end of 2026.

title: European Union — High-Risk Systems Secured
content:
Country: European Union
Organization: European Commission
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2030–2030
Title: High-Risk Systems Secured
Description: Critical infrastructure and high-risk systems secured with PQC by 2030.

title: European Union — Full EU PQC Transition
content:
Country: European Union
Organization: European Commission
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2035–2035
Title: Full EU PQC Transition
Description: Complete PQC transition for all feasible systems across EU member states.

title: France — Initial PQC Position Paper
content:
Country: France
Organization: Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2022–2022
Title: Initial PQC Position Paper
Description: ANSSI publishes initial position paper on post-quantum cryptography transition, mandating hybrid approach.

title: France — PQC Position Paper Follow-up
content:
Country: France
Organization: Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2023–2023
Title: PQC Position Paper Follow-up
Description: ANSSI publishes follow-up position paper with updated guidance on hybridization requirements.

title: France — Joint QKD Position Paper
content:
Country: France
Organization: ANSSI, BSI, NLNCSA, Swedish NCSA
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2024–2024
Title: Joint QKD Position Paper
Description: ANSSI with BSI, NLNCSA, and Swedish NCSA publish joint Position Paper on Quantum Key Distribution. Recommends prioritizing PQC over QKD. January 26, 2024.

title: France — PQC Market Studies Published
content:
Country: France
Organization: Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information
Type: Milestone | Phase: Research
Period: 2024–2024
Title: PQC Market Studies Published
Description: ANSSI publishes two market studies on PQC readiness among French cybersecurity ecosystem (developers and consultants). Findings show significant preparedness gaps. November 25, 2024.

title: France — Phase 1 - Pre-Quantum Security
content:
Country: France
Organization: Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2024–2025
Title: Phase 1 - Pre-Quantum Security
Description: Mandatory pre-quantum security measures; PQC implementation optional but encouraged.

title: France — Cryptographic Accreditation Doctrine Updated
content:
Country: France
Organization: Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Cryptographic Accreditation Doctrine Updated
Description: ANSSI Centre de Certification National (CCN) updates cryptographic accreditation doctrine to include PQC evaluation requirements. March 2025.

title: France — CEA-Leti CESTI PQC Accreditation
content:
Country: France
Organization: Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2025–2025
Title: CEA-Leti CESTI PQC Accreditation
Description: CEA-Leti becomes first CESTI (evaluation center) accredited by ANSSI to evaluate PQC solutions. September 29, 2025.

title: France — First PQC Security Visas Issued
content:
Country: France
Organization: Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2025–2025
Title: First PQC Security Visas Issued
Description: ANSSI issues first French PQC security certifications: Thales Smartcard MultiApp 5.2 Premium PQC (Sept 29) and Samsung S3SSE2A microcontroller (Oct 1). Both include lattice-based algorithms.

title: France — PQC FAQ Published
content:
Country: France
Organization: Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: PQC FAQ Published
Description: ANSSI publishes comprehensive PQC FAQ covering algorithm recommendations (ML-KEM, ML-DSA, FN-DSA, SLH-DSA), hybridization requirements, and transition timeline. October 2025.

title: France — PG-083 Cryptographic Guide Update (Planned)
content:
Country: France
Organization: Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: PG-083 Cryptographic Guide Update (Planned)
Description: ANSSI plans to update Guide des mécanismes cryptographiques (PG-083) and algorithm selection guide to include PQC by end of 2025.

title: France — Phase 2 - Hybridization Required
content:
Country: France
Organization: Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2025–2030
Title: Phase 2 - Hybridization Required
Description: Hybrid cryptography (classical + PQC) required for systems requiring post-quantum security assurance.

title: France — IPsec DR Referential Update (Planned)
content:
Country: France
Organization: Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2026–2026
Title: IPsec DR Referential Update (Planned)
Description: ANSSI plans revision of IPsec DR referential to include PQC requirements. Expected 2026.

title: France — PQC Qualification Requirement
content:
Country: France
Organization: Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2027–2027
Title: PQC Qualification Requirement
Description: PQC integration mandatory for cryptographic solutions entering ANSSI qualification process, at least for certain product types. Starting 2027.

title: France — Phase 3 - Standalone PQC Optional

Country: NATO

content:
Country: New Zealand

Title: Planning Phase

content:
Country: New Zealand

Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025

content:
Country: New Zealand

Type: Phase | Phase: Migration

title: Singapore — Financial Sector Planning
content:
Country: Singapore
Organization: Cyber Security Agency / Monetary Authority of Singapore
Type: Phase | Phase: Discovery
Period: 2024–2026

title: Singapore — Quantum-Safe Handbook and Quantum Readiness Index Released
content:
Country: Singapore
Organization: Cyber Security Agency / GovTech / IMDA
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Quantum-Safe Handbook and Quantum Readiness Index Released
Description: CSA, GovTech, and IMDA jointly release draft Quantum-Safe Handbook and Quantum Readiness Index at SICW 2025 for public consultation (Oct 23 – Dec 31 2025). Handbook provides four-pillar PQC migration framework for CII owners: cryptographic asset discovery, risk prioritisation, phased migration, and post-migration monitoring. QRI is a five-domain self-assessment tool (Governance, Risk Assessment, Training, External Engagement, Technology and Agility).

title: Singapore — Financial Sector QKD Sandbox Completed
content:
Country: Singapore
Organization: Monetary Authority of Singapore
Type: Milestone | Phase: Testing
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Financial Sector QKD Sandbox Completed
Description: MAS completes QKD proof-of-concept sandbox (Sept 2024-March 2025). DBS, HSBC, OCBC, UOB tested QKD for settlement files. Generated 6.75M AES-256 keys/day per bank.

title: South Korea — KpqC Competition Results
content:
Country: South Korea
Organization: National Intelligence Service / KISA
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2025–2025
Title: KpqC Competition Results

Country: South Korea

Country: Spain

Country: Taiwan
Organization: Ministry of Digital Affairs

Period: 2027–2027

content:

Period: 2024–2024

Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2024–2025
Title: Web/Cloud Services Transition

Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency

Country: United States
Organization: Executive Office of the President

Period: 2025–2025

Country: United States
Organization: Executive Office of the President
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Executive Order 14306 Issued

Country: United States
Organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2025–2025

Country: United States
Organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2025–2025

Description: NIST holds Sixth PQC Standardization Conference in September 2025 to discuss algorithm updates, FIPS 206, and migration guidance.

Organization: NIST National Cybersecurity Center of Excellence

Period: 2025–2025

Description: NCCoE publishes initial draft of Cybersecurity White Paper 48 - Mappings of Migration to PQC Project Capabilities to Risk Framework Documents (CSF 2.0, SP 800-53). Comment period closed October 20, 2025.

content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2025–2027

Period: 2025–2027

title: United States — PQC Interoperability Testing
content:
Country: United States
Organization: NIST National Cybersecurity Center of Excellence
Type: Phase | Phase: Testing
Period: 2025–2028
Title: PQC Interoperability Testing
Description: NCCoE performs interoperability testing of standardized PQC algorithms in common protocols.

title: United States — Large PKI Systems Migration
content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2025–2033
Title: Large PKI Systems Migration
Description: Migration of large public key infrastructure systems to CNSA 2.0 algorithms.

title: United States — HQC Standard Finalized (Expected)
content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2027–2027
Title: HQC Standard Finalized (Expected)
Description: Final standard incorporating HQC algorithm expected to be published approximately one year after draft.

title: United States — NSS Acquisitions CNSA 2.0 Required
content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2027–2027
Title: NSS Acquisitions CNSA 2.0 Required
Description: New National Security Systems acquisitions must be CNSA 2.0 compliant starting January 2027.

title: United States — 112-bit Security Deprecated
content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2030–2030
Title: 112-bit Security Deprecated
Description: Algorithms at 112-bit security strength (e.g., RSA-2048, DH-2048, ECDH P-256) deprecated per NIST IR 8547 (draft). Disallowed in federal systems after 2035. Systems must migrate to 128-bit minimum (AES-256, SHA-384+) or approved PQC algorithms.

title: United States — CNSA 2.0 Exclusive - Network/Signing
content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2030–2030
Title: CNSA 2.0 Exclusive - Network/Signing
Description: Exclusively use CNSA 2.0 for software/firmware signing and traditional networking equipment.

title: United States — NSS Quantum Resistant Majority
content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2031–2031
Title: NSS Quantum Resistant Majority
Description: Vast majority of cryptography in National Security Systems should be quantum resistant.

title: United States — CNSA 2.0 Exclusive - OS/Web/Cloud
content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2033–2033
Title: CNSA 2.0 Exclusive - OS/Web/Cloud
Description: Exclusively use CNSA 2.0 for web browsers/servers, cloud services, operating systems, and large PKI systems.

title: United States — Full CNSA 2.0 Transition Complete
content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2035–2035
Title: Full CNSA 2.0 Transition Complete
Description: Complete transition to CNSA 2.0 algorithms for all National Security Systems (NSS).

title: United States — CISA PQC Product Category List Publication
content:
Country: United States
Organization: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Regulation
Period: 2025–2025
Title: CISA PQC Product Category List Publication
Description: CISA released initial list of product categories supporting PQC per Executive Order. List developed with ATARC covering data management, networking, and enterprise endpoint security. Products must be generally available, FIPS-compliant, and interoperable.

title: United States — CNSA 1.0 Compliance Deadline for NSS
content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2025–2025
Title: CNSA 1.0 Compliance Deadline for NSS
Description: All existing National Security Systems must be CNSA 1.0 compliant  or submit waiver request by end of year. Systems not compliant have 6 months to achieve CNSA 2.0 compliance or request waiver.

title: International — PKI Consortium PQC Conference 2025 Conclusions
content:
Country: International
Organization: Public Key Infrastructure Consortium
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: PKI Consortium PQC Conference 2025 Conclusions
Description: World's largest PQC conference concluded in Kuala Lumpur with 2500+ delegates from 30+ countries. Issued urgent global call for immediate action on PQC migration. Next conference announced for Germany 2026.

title: United States — NIST PQC Algorithms in Windows Platform
content:
Country: United States
Organization: Microsoft Corporation
Type: Milestone | Phase: Migration
Period: 2025–2025
Title: NIST PQC Algorithms in Windows Platform
Description: Microsoft announced general availability of NIST post-quantum cryptography algorithms ML-KEM and ML-DSA in Windows 11 and Windows Server 2025 via November 2025 update  enabling enterprise PQC adoption.

title: United States — US Post-Quantum Root of Trust Service Launch
content:
Country: United States
Organization: SEALSQ Corp
Type: Milestone | Phase: Migration
Period: 2025–2025
Title: US Post-Quantum Root of Trust Service Launch
Description: SEALSQ launched US-based Post-Quantum Root of Trust service for enterprises and government agencies aligned with NIST PQC standards and NSA CNSA 2.0 framework integrating with Quantum Shield QS7001 hardware.

title: International — ML-KEM Embedded Secure Element Breakthrough
content:
Country: International
Organization: Samsung System LSI and Thales
Type: Milestone | Phase: Migration
Period: 2026–2026
Title: ML-KEM Embedded Secure Element Breakthrough
Description: Samsung System LSI and Thales announce breakthrough in embedded security bringing ML-KEM quantum-resistant capabilities to tiny secure elements in electronic devices. First commercial PQC implementation for IoT and embedded systems.

title: International — RFC 9794 PQC Hybrid Terminology
content:
Country: International
Organization: Internet Engineering Task Force
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2025–2025
Title: RFC 9794 PQC Hybrid Terminology
Description: IETF publishes RFC 9794 Terminology for Post-Quantum Traditional Hybrid Schemes. Standardizes definitions for hybrid PQC/classical cryptographic schemes. Over 20 IETF drafts now reference this standard.

title: International — PQC Inventory Workbook Published
content:
Country: International
Organization: Post-Quantum Cryptography Coalition
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: PQC Inventory Workbook Published
Description: PQCC publishes PQC Inventory Workbook to assist organizations in creating centralized inventory for tracking cryptographic migration efforts. Follows May 2025 PQC Migration Roadmap. Founded by IBM, Microsoft, MITRE, PQShield, SandboxAQ.

title: Global — Helios Quantum Computer Commercial Launch
content:
Country: Global
Organization: Quantinuum
Type: Milestone | Phase: POC
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Helios Quantum Computer Commercial Launch
Description: Quantinuum launches Helios commercial quantum computer with 98 all-to-all connected qubits and industry-leading fidelity (99.9975% single-qubit 99.921% two-qubit gate fidelity). Designed to enable Generative Quantum AI.

title: United States — National Quantum Initiative Reauthorization Act
content:
Country: United States
Organization: United States Congress
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2026–2026
Title: National Quantum Initiative Reauthorization Act
Description: Bipartisan legislation introduced to extend National Quantum Initiative by 5 years to December 2034. Authorizes $85M/yr for NIST quantum centers, creates 5 NSF Multidisciplinary Centers for Quantum Research. Endorsed by IBM Microsoft Google IonQ.

title: G7 — Financial Sector PQC Roadmap
content:
Country: G7
Organization: G7 Cyber Expert Group
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Financial Sector PQC Roadmap
Description: G7 Cyber Expert Group (chaired by US Treasury and Bank of England) releases Advancing a Coordinated Roadmap for PQC Transition in Financial Sector. Recommends critical financial systems quantum-safe by 2030-2032, non-critical by 2035.

title: United States — Federal PQC Procurement Guidance
content:
Country: United States
Organization: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Federal PQC Procurement Guidance
Description: CISA issues federal buying guidance per EO 14306 directing agencies to acquire only PQC-capable products in widely available categories: cloud services, collaboration tools, browsers, and endpoint security. Networking and identity management still transitioning.

title: Global — Post-Quantum Security Top Priority
content:
Country: Global
Organization: Ethereum Foundation
Type: Milestone | Phase: Migration
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Post-Quantum Security Top Priority
Description: Ethereum Foundation elevates post-quantum security to top strategic priority. Forms dedicated PQ team, launches $2M in bounties (Poseidon Prize $1M and Proximity Prize $1M), and begins bi-weekly PQ All Core Devs calls February 2026.

title: Singapore — Helios Quantum Computer Singapore Deployment
content:
Country: Singapore
Organization: Quantinuum and Singapore National Quantum Office
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Helios Quantum Computer Singapore Deployment
Description: Quantinuum partners with Singapore National Quantum Office to deploy Helios quantum computer (98 qubits) first deployment outside US. R&D and Operations Centre established at one-north technology district.

title: International — ETSI/IQC QSC Conference 2026
content:
Country: International
Organization: ETSI and University of Waterloo IQC
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2026–2026
Title: ETSI/IQC QSC Conference 2026
Description: 11th ETSI/IQC Quantum Safe Cryptography Conference announced for Ottawa, hosted by Carleton University June 16-18. Topics include PQC deployments, QKD, migration challenges, and cryptographic agility.

title: Hong Kong — Critical Infrastructure Cybersecurity Ordinance
content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Legislative Council of Hong Kong
Type: Milestone | Phase: Regulation
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Critical Infrastructure Cybersecurity Ordinance
Description: Protection of Critical Infrastructure (Computer System) Ordinance passed by Legislative Council on March 19 2025. Effective January 1 2026. Covers 8 sectors: energy IT banking air transport land transport maritime healthcare and telecommunications.

title: Hong Kong — HKMA Fintech Adoption Report: PQC Commitment
content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Hong Kong Monetary Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2025–2025
Title: HKMA Fintech Adoption Report: PQC Commitment
Description: HKMA publishes Fintech Adoption: Progress and Future Directions report (Jan 2025 Tech Maturity Stock-take of 56 authorized institutions). Finds only 7% of Hong Kong banks engaged with quantum computing. Commits to promoting post-quantum cryptography as primary quantum-safety mechanism. Plans to highlight importance of quantum safety and practical PQC adoption for the financial sector.

title: Hong Kong — Fintech 2030 Strategy: PQC Resilience Pillar
content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Hong Kong Monetary Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Fintech 2030 Strategy: PQC Resilience Pillar
Description: HKMA unveils Fintech 2030 strategy at Hong Kong FinTech Week 2025. The Resilience pillar explicitly commits to driving industry readiness for post-quantum cryptography and building quantum-safe infrastructure for secured financial services. Over 40 initiatives under four pillars: Data (D), AI (A), Resilience (R), and Tokenisation (T).

title: Hong Kong — Fintech Promotion Blueprint: Quantum Preparedness Index
content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Hong Kong Monetary Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Fintech Promotion Blueprint: Quantum Preparedness Index
Description: HKMA unveils Fintech Promotion Blueprint including Quantum Preparedness Index as flagship project to assess banking sector PQC readiness and set measurable migration targets. The Index will guide institutions through PQC transition journey with special focus on supporting small and medium-sized banks.

title: Hong Kong — Financial Sector PQC Planning
content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Hong Kong Monetary Authority
Type: Phase | Phase: Discovery
Period: 2026–2028
Title: Financial Sector PQC Planning
Description: Banks develop quantum preparedness plans and cryptographic inventories per HKMA Fintech 2030 Quantum Preparedness Index. Industry consultation on PQC adoption guidelines underway.

title: Hong Kong — Fintech Promotion Blueprint Published
content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Hong Kong Monetary Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Fintech Promotion Blueprint Published
Description: HKMA publishes Fintech Promotion Blueprint (From Adoption to Advancement) on February 3, 2026, formalizing the Quantum Preparedness Index as a flagship initiative to measure banking sector PQC readiness and set measurable quantum-safe migration targets.

title: Singapore — NQSN+ Launched
content:
Country: Singapore
Organization: Infocomm Media Development Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: POC
Period: 2023–2023
Title: NQSN+ Launched
Description: Deputy PM Heng Swee Keat launches National Quantum-Safe Network Plus (NQSN+) at ATxSG 2023. Businesses can integrate QKD and PQC solutions through NQSN+ operators. IMDA publishes Singapore first local QKD network standard.

title: Singapore — Cross-Border PQC Experiment
content:
Country: Singapore
Organization: Monetary Authority of Singapore and Banque de France
Type: Milestone | Phase: Testing
Period: 2024–2024
Title: Cross-Border PQC Experiment
Description: MAS and Banque de France conduct groundbreaking cross-border post-quantum cryptography experiment to enhance communication security between central banks using PQC algorithms.

title: India — National Quantum-Safe Roadmap Published
content:
Country: India
Organization: Department of Science and Technology National Quantum Mission
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2026–2026
Title: National Quantum-Safe Roadmap Published
Description: DST Task Force publishes comprehensive phased roadmap for PQC migration. CII foundations by 2027 high-priority migration by 2028 full CII quantum-safe by 2029 nationwide adoption by 2033. Mandates Cryptographic Bill of Materials (CBOM) submissions from FY 2027-28.

title: India — CII PQC Foundations Deadline
content:
Country: India
Organization: Department of Science and Technology National Quantum Mission
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2027–2027
Title: CII PQC Foundations Deadline
Description: Critical Information Infrastructure in defense power telecom space and core government must have PQC adoption foundations in place. Mandatory CBOM submissions begin. Testing labs (TEC STQC BIS) to be operational by December 2026.

title: India — National PQC Migration
content:
Country: India
Organization: Department of Science and Technology National Quantum Mission
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2027–2033
Title: National PQC Migration
Description: Phased migration: high-priority CII systems by 2028 full CII quantum-safe by 2029 all sectors by 2033. Hybrid PQC plus QKD recommended for high-assurance applications. Quantum-safe requirements integrated into procurement.

title: India — Full Nationwide PQC Adoption
content:
Country: India
Organization: Department of Science and Technology National Quantum Mission
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2033–2033
Title: Full Nationwide PQC Adoption
Description: Target completion for PQC migration across all sectors nationwide under National Quantum Mission roadmap.

title: Japan — Government PQC Transition Deadline
content:
Country: Japan
Organization: National Cyber Command Office
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2035–2035
Title: Government PQC Transition Deadline
Description: Japan National Cyber Command Office sets 2035 as deadline for government agencies to complete PQC transition. Aligns with US UK and Canada timelines. Detailed implementation roadmap planned for FY2026. Recommends cryptographic agility and hybrid schemes during transition.

title: Malaysia — National PQC Migration Plan Published
content:
Country: Malaysia
Organization: National Cyber Security Agency of Malaysia
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2025–2025
Title: National PQC Migration Plan Published
Description: Malaysia Ministry of Digital and NACSA publish National PQC Migration Plan (2025-2030) and launch PQC Sandbox 2025 proof-of-concept program at PKI Consortium PQC Conference in Kuala Lumpur. Positions Malaysia as ASEAN PQC leader.

title: Malaysia — National PQC Migration
content:
Country: Malaysia
Organization: National Cyber Security Agency of Malaysia
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2025–2030
Title: National PQC Migration
Description: Phased national PQC migration with proof-of-concept sandbox program. First ASEAN nation to publish a comprehensive national PQC migration roadmap.

title: United Arab Emirates — NIST Additional Signatures Round 2 Contributions
content:
Country: United Arab Emirates
Organization: Technology Innovation Institute
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2024–2024
Title: NIST Additional Signatures Round 2 Contributions
Description: TII Cryptography Research Center contributes to 6 of 14 NIST Round 2 additional digital signature schemes: LESS Mirath PERK RYDE SDitH and SQISign. TII also contributed to FN-DSA (FALCON) standardization as FIPS 206.

title: United Arab Emirates — Dubai PQC Guideline Launched
content:
Country: United Arab Emirates
Organization: Dubai Electronic Security Center
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Dubai PQC Guideline Launched
Description: DESC launches Post-Quantum Cryptography Guideline at GISEC Global 2025 to prepare Dubai digital infrastructure against quantum threats. Also launches Zero Trust Assessment Tool and Ethaq Plus digital trust platform.

title: United Arab Emirates — Quantum-Secure Communications Testbed
content:
Country: United Arab Emirates
Organization: Abu Dhabi Global Market and Technology Innovation Institute
Type: Milestone | Phase: POC
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Quantum-Secure Communications Testbed
Description: UAE first quantum-secure communications testbed: 3-node QKD network deployed across Abu Dhabi Global Market financial centre. Joint initiative by ADGM TII Hub71 and ASPIRE in live commercial environment.

title: United Arab Emirates — National Encryption Policy Approved
content:
Country: United Arab Emirates
Organization: UAE Cybersecurity Council
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2025–2025
Title: National Encryption Policy Approved
Description: UAE Cybersecurity Council approves National Encryption Policy and Executive Regulation at CyberQ 2025 summit. Mandates all government entities develop PQC transition plans submit migration roadmaps by 2026 and maintain automated cryptographic asset inventories with crypto agility by design.

title: United Arab Emirates — National PQC Migration Program
content:
Country: United Arab Emirates
Organization: UAE Cybersecurity Council
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2026–2030
Title: National PQC Migration Program
Description: QuantumGate partnership drives large-scale PQC implementation across National Information Assurance Program National Cybersecurity Index Platform and National Post-Quantum Migration Program. Crypto Discovery Tool and QSphere VPN deployed across government.

title: Saudi Arabia — ECC-2:2024 Cybersecurity Controls
content:
Country: Saudi Arabia
Organization: National Cybersecurity Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: Regulation
Period: 2024–2024
Title: ECC-2:2024 Cybersecurity Controls
Description: NCA releases Essential Cybersecurity Controls 2024 (ECC-2:2024) with expanded cryptography domain covering encryption strength key management secure protocols and lifecycle governance. New NCA Regulations grant enforcement authority.

title: Saudi Arabia — Quantum Economy Landscape Report
content:
Country: Saudi Arabia
Organization: Center for the Fourth Industrial Revolution
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2024–2024
Title: Quantum Economy Landscape Report
Description: Saudi C4IR publishes Quantum Economy Landscape in Saudi Arabia report. First country to pilot WEF Quantum Economy Blueprint. National quantum strategy with $200M initial budget aligned with Vision 2030.

title: Saudi Arabia — First Quantum Computer in KSA
content:
Country: Saudi Arabia
Organization: Saudi Aramco and Pasqal
Type: Milestone | Phase: Research
Period: 2025–2025
Title: First Quantum Computer in KSA
Description: Aramco and Pasqal deploy Saudi Arabia first quantum computer (200 qubits neutral atom) at Aramco data center. Region first quantum computer dedicated to industrial applications. Plans to upgrade to hybrid analog-digital mode.

title: Bahrain — National Quantum-Safe Deployment
content:
Country: Bahrain
Organization: National Cyber Security Centre
Type: Milestone | Phase: Migration
Period: 2025–2025
Title: National Quantum-Safe Deployment
Description: Bahrain NCSC partners with SandboxAQ to deploy AQtive Guard AI-powered cryptographic security platform across 60+ government ministries. One of world first large-scale national commitments to quantum-safe cybersecurity.

title: Jordan — Financial Sector PQC Roadmap
content:
Country: Jordan
Organization: Central Bank of Jordan
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Financial Sector PQC Roadmap
Description: Central Bank of Jordan publishes sector-wide roadmap for transitioning banks to quantum-resistant encryption. Phased approach: cryptographic asset cataloging risk-based prioritization governance integration sandbox testing and full transition.

content:

title: Post-Quantum Key Encapsulation Mechanisms (PQ KEMs) for JOSE and COSE
content:
Title: Post-Quantum Key Encapsulation Mechanisms (PQ KEMs) for JOSE and COSE
Description: Defines how to use ML-KEM and other PQC KEMs within JOSE/JWE and COSE/COSE_Encrypt for quantum-safe token encryption. Introduces new alg identifiers for ML-KEM-512, ML-KEM-768, ML-KEM-1024 in JWE and COSE.
Type: Protocol | Status: Active Internet-Draft
Authors: IETF JOSE WG
Algorithm Family: Lattice-based
Security Levels: L1,L3,L5
Migration Urgency: High
Industries: IT; Software Development; APIs; Identity Management; Finance
Region: International
Published: 2024-06-01 | Updated: 2025-12-09

title: JSON Web Token Best Current Practices
content:
Title: JSON Web Token Best Current Practices
Description: Documents security pitfalls and best practices for JWT usage including alg confusion attacks, none-algorithm vulnerabilities, audience validation, and expiry enforcement. Essential pre-PQC hardening baseline before migrating JWT signing to PQC algorithms.
Type: Guidance | Status: Best Current Practice
Authors: IETF OAUTH WG
Algorithm Family: Classical
Security Levels: N/A
Migration Urgency: Medium

title: Aerospace / Aviation — Aircraft avionics cryptographic exposure: RTCA DO-326A defines airworthiness sec
content:
Industry: Aerospace / Aviation
Threat: Aircraft avionics cryptographic exposure: RTCA DO-326A defines airworthiness security processes for aircraft systems with 30-40 year service lives. ARINC 664 (AFDX) network security and flight management systems rely on RSA/ECDSA. Aircraft entering service today will operate well beyond CRQC arrival with no practical mid-life cryptographic retrofit path.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: Avionics PKI, ARINC 664 network security, flight management system authentication
PQC Replacement: Crypto-agile avionics design, ML-DSA for flight software signing, hybrid certificate chains
Source: RTCA DO-326A / DO-356A Airworthiness Security

title: Aerospace / Aviation — Satellite communication HNDL vulnerability: Military and commercial satellites h
content:
Industry: Aerospace / Aviation
Threat: Satellite communication HNDL vulnerability: Military and commercial satellites have 15-25 year operational lifespans. NSA CNSA 2.0 mandates PQC for all national security systems including satellite uplink/downlink by 2035. Currently intercepted satellite communications are harvestable for future quantum decryption.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: Satellite uplink/downlink RSA/ECDSA, ground station TLS, inter-satellite link encryption
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87 per CNSA 2.0, AES-256
Source: NSA CNSA 2.0 Cybersecurity Advisory

title: Aerospace / Aviation — Air traffic management authentication gap: ICAO Assembly Resolution A41-19 (2022
content:
Industry: Aerospace / Aviation
Threat: Air traffic management authentication gap: ICAO Assembly Resolution A41-19 (2022) addresses aviation cybersecurity but ADS-B transmissions remain unencrypted and unauthenticated. ADS-B currently lacks any cryptographic protection; quantum computing becomes relevant when authenticated ADS-B is deployed using public-key cryptography, which would itself require PQC to remain secure.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: ADS-B (unencrypted), Mode S, ATC data links, ACARS messaging
PQC Replacement: Authenticated ADS-B with ML-DSA, quantum-safe ATC protocols
Source: ICAO Assembly Resolution A41-19

title: Automotive / Connected Vehicles — V2X PKI quantum transition: ISO/SAE 21434 (published August 2021) establishes cy
content:
Industry: Automotive / Connected Vehicles
Threat: V2X PKI quantum transition: ISO/SAE 21434 (published August 2021) establishes cybersecurity engineering requirements for road vehicles. V2X communications use ECDSA P-256 certificates per IEEE 1609.2. Vehicles have 12+ year lifecycles meaning 2026 production vehicles remain on roads through 2038+ when CRQCs may be operational.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: ECDSA P-256 V2X certificates, IEEE 1609.2 PKI, digital key infrastructure
PQC Replacement: ML-DSA V2X certificates, hybrid V2X authentication, FN-DSA for constrained ECUs
Source: ISO/SAE 21434 Road Vehicle Cybersecurity

title: Automotive / Connected Vehicles — OTA firmware update signature forgery: Over-the-Air update mechanisms for modern
content:
Industry: Automotive / Connected Vehicles
Threat: OTA firmware update signature forgery: Over-the-Air update mechanisms for modern connected vehicles use RSA/ECDSA code signing. Quantum forgery of update signatures enables malicious code injection into vehicle ECUs controlling braking, steering, and powertrain systems.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: RSA/ECDSA code signing, secure boot chains, ECU firmware verification
PQC Replacement: ML-DSA firmware signing, SLH-DSA for safety-critical ECUs, dual-signature verification
Source: SAE J3061 / ISO/SAE 21434 Vehicle Cybersecurity

title: Automotive / Connected Vehicles — UNECE WP.29 R155/R156 cybersecurity regulation gap: Mandatory for new vehicle ty
content:
Industry: Automotive / Connected Vehicles
Threat: UNECE WP.29 R155/R156 cybersecurity regulation gap: Mandatory for new vehicle type approvals in EU since July 2022 and extended to all new vehicles July 2024. Requires cybersecurity management systems and secure OTA updates but does not yet address post-quantum cryptography creating a future compliance gap.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: TLS for telematics, RSA/ECDSA for OTA signing, connected car cloud API authentication
PQC Replacement: Hybrid TLS 1.3 with ML-KEM, ML-DSA OTA signing, crypto-agile telematics
Source: UNECE WP.29 Regulations R155/R156

title: Automotive / Connected Vehicles — In-vehicle network security quantum exposure: ISO/SAE 21434 covers the full vehi
content:
Industry: Automotive / Connected Vehicles
Threat: In-vehicle network security quantum exposure: ISO/SAE 21434 covers the full vehicle cybersecurity lifecycle including CAN bus, LIN, FlexRay, and automotive Ethernet backbone networks. Embedded ECUs have limited computational resources and constrained crypto-agility, making mid-lifecycle PQC migration impractical for vehicles already in production.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: CAN bus authentication, secure boot in ECUs, in-vehicle Ethernet encryption, HSM key storage
PQC Replacement: Hardware security modules with PQC support, crypto-agile ECU design, ML-DSA for secure boot
Source: ISO/SAE 21434 Road Vehicle Cybersecurity

title: Cloud Computing / Data Centers — Cloud backup and disaster recovery HNDL risk: Air-gapped backups and long-term a
content:
Industry: Cloud Computing / Data Centers
Threat: Cloud backup and disaster recovery HNDL risk: Air-gapped backups and long-term archival storage encrypted with RSA/ECDSA key wrapping are vulnerable to harvest-now-decrypt-later. Cloud backup data often retained for 7+ years for compliance.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: Backup encryption key wrapping, archival storage TLS, disaster recovery replication
PQC Replacement: PQC-enabled backup encryption, ML-KEM key wrapping, AES-256 for archival
Source: Cloud Security Alliance Quantum-Safe Guidelines

title: Cloud Computing / Data Centers — Cloud Security Alliance quantum readiness guidance: CSA published quantum-safe s
content:
Industry: Cloud Computing / Data Centers
Threat: Cloud Security Alliance quantum readiness guidance: CSA published quantum-safe security guidance identifying crypto-agility as critical requirement for cloud deployments. Multi-cloud environments using 5+ key management systems face fragmented PQC migration paths.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: Cloud KMS key wrapping, multi-cloud encryption, BYOK/HYOK solutions, secrets management
PQC Replacement: Unified PQC key management, ML-KEM cloud HSM integration, crypto-agile KMS
Source: Cloud Security Alliance Quantum-Safe Security

title: Cloud Computing / Data Centers — Federal cloud PQC compliance requirements: NIST SP 800-210 general access contro
content:
Industry: Cloud Computing / Data Centers
Threat: Federal cloud PQC compliance requirements: NIST SP 800-210 general access control guidance for cloud systems combined with NIST IR 8547 deprecation timeline means federal cloud deployments must transition to PQC by 2030. FedRAMP and StateRAMP certifications will require FIPS 203/204/205 compliance.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: Federal cloud TLS, FedRAMP-authorized encryption, cloud HSM FIPS modules
PQC Replacement: FIPS 203 (ML-KEM), FIPS 204 (ML-DSA), FIPS 205 (SLH-DSA) cloud implementations
Source: NIST IR 8547 / NIST SP 800-210

title: Cloud Computing / Data Centers — Cloud HSM and key management PQC gap: NIST SP 800-210 defines access control for
content:
Industry: Cloud Computing / Data Centers
Threat: Cloud HSM and key management PQC gap: NIST SP 800-210 defines access control for cloud systems but cloud-hosted HSMs and KMS services currently lack full PQC support. FIPS 140-3 validation for PQC modules is ongoing with multi-year certification timelines. Organizations using cloud HSMs face vendor dependency for PQC migration.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: Cloud HSM root keys, KMS key wrapping, tenant isolation keys, FIPS 140-3 modules
PQC Replacement: FIPS 140-3 validated PQC HSM modules, bring-your-own-key PQC, ML-KEM key wrapping
Source: NIST SP 800-210 Cloud Access Control

title: Cross-Industry — Quantum-safe readiness gap: Organizations score average 25/100 on IBM Quantum-Sa
content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: Quantum-safe readiness gap: Organizations score average 25/100 on IBM Quantum-Safe Readiness Index. 70% lack complete cryptographic inventory, creating massive vulnerability window. Estimated 5-6 year gap between CRQC arrival and organizational readiness completion.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: All asymmetric cryptography, PKI infrastructure, key management systems
PQC Replacement: Comprehensive CBOM, automated discovery tools, ML-KEM/ML-DSA migration
Source: IBM Quantum-Safe Readiness Index 2025

title: Cross-Industry — NIST IR 8547 proposed transition timeline: NIST Initial Public Draft (November 2
content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: NIST IR 8547 proposed transition timeline: NIST Initial Public Draft (November 2024) establishes proposed timeline to deprecate RSA, ECDSA, ECDH, and EdDSA by 2030 and disallow them entirely after 2035. Sets 112-bit security minimum. Final version pending as of February 2026. Landmark publication making PQC migration a compliance requirement for federal systems.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048/3072/4096, ECDSA P-256/P-384, ECDH, EdDSA
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-768/1024 (FIPS 203), ML-DSA-44/65/87 (FIPS 204), SLH-DSA (FIPS 205)
Source: NIST IR 8547 Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography Standards

title: Cross-Industry — Vendor dependency crisis: 62% of organizations incorrectly assume vendors will m
content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: Vendor dependency crisis: 62% of organizations incorrectly assume vendors will manage quantum-safe transition automatically. Lack of contractual PQC requirements and service-level agreements for crypto-agility.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: Third-party API keys, vendor-managed HSMs, cloud KMS
PQC Replacement: Vendor PQC roadmap requirements, contractual crypto-agility clauses
Source: IBM Quantum-Safe Readiness Index 2025

title: Cross-Industry — CRQC timeline uncertainty: Global Risk Institute 2024 survey of 32 quantum exper
content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: CRQC timeline uncertainty: Global Risk Institute 2024 survey of 32 quantum experts estimates 19-34% probability of a cryptographically relevant quantum computer (CRQC) within 10 years and 5-14% within 5 years. Most credible estimates place CRQC arrival in the 2030-2040 range with wide uncertainty. Harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks make immediate PQC migration urgent regardless of exact timeline.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048/4096, ECC P-256/384, ECDSA, ECDH, EdDSA
PQC Replacement: Immediate hybrid PQC deployment, ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87
Source: Global Risk Institute Quantum Threat Timeline

title: Cross-Industry — FIPS 206 FN-DSA (Falcon) standardization: NIST submitted draft FIPS 206 for appr
content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: FIPS 206 FN-DSA (Falcon) standardization: NIST submitted draft FIPS 206 for approval August 2025. FN-DSA is the 4th PQC algorithm to be standardized, selected for use cases where compact signature size is critical (root and intermediate certificates, constrained environments). Based on NTRU lattices using FFT. Expected IPD release for public comment followed by finalization in late 2026 or 2027.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: RSA/ECDSA certificates requiring compact signatures, root CA keys, constrained device authentication
PQC Replacement: FN-DSA (FIPS 206) for compact signatures, ML-DSA for general-purpose signing
Source: NIST FIPS 206 FN-DSA Status

title: Cross-Industry — HQC selected as 5th PQC algorithm: NIST announced March 11 2025 the selection of
content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: HQC selected as 5th PQC algorithm: NIST announced March 11 2025 the selection of HQC (Hamming Quasi-Cyclic) as a backup KEM to ML-KEM. HQC is based on error-correcting codes rather than lattices, providing algorithmic diversity in case a lattice-based vulnerability is discovered. Draft standard expected for public comment in early 2026 with final standard in 2027.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: All systems relying solely on ML-KEM without backup KEM
PQC Replacement: HQC as backup KEM alongside ML-KEM-768/1024, algorithm diversity strategy
Source: NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography Standardization

title: Cross-Industry — EU coordinated PQC transition roadmap: European Commission published coordinated
content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: EU coordinated PQC transition roadmap: European Commission published coordinated PQC roadmap (June 2025) establishing EU-wide milestones. By December 2026: national transition roadmaps and initial identification steps. By December 2030: high-risk use cases transitioned and PQC-by-default for new systems. By December 2035: full PQC transition complete across all EU member states.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: All quantum-vulnerable cryptography in EU member state systems and critical infrastructure
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, hybrid implementations per EU coordinated timeline
Source: European Commission Digital Strategy — PQC Roadmap

title: Cross-Industry — NIST SP 800-227 KEM recommendations: Published September 2025 as final guidance
content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: NIST SP 800-227 KEM recommendations: Published September 2025 as final guidance on key-encapsulation mechanisms. Provides definitions, security properties, and implementation recommendations for KEMs as companion guidance to FIPS 203 (ML-KEM). Establishes best practices for KEM usage in protocols, hybrid constructions, and key management — essential reference for correct PQC deployment.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: Incorrect or insecure KEM implementations, legacy key exchange protocols
PQC Replacement: FIPS 203 ML-KEM per SP 800-227 guidance, proper KEM-TLS integration
Source: NIST SP 800-227 Recommendations for KEMs

title: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain — Bitcoin ECDSA transaction hijacking: Approximately $718B in quantum-vulnerable P
content:
Industry: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain
Threat: Bitcoin ECDSA transaction hijacking: Approximately $718B in quantum-vulnerable P2PK addresses with exposed public keys (price-dependent estimate). Early P2PK addresses including Satoshi's estimated 1.1M BTC are permanently vulnerable as public keys are exposed on-chain. P2PKH addresses are protected until first spend.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: secp256k1 ECDSA
PQC Replacement: P2QRH BIP proposal, Lamport signatures, hash-based migration
Source: Federal Reserve HNDL Paper

title: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain — Ethereum Foundation PQC initiative: Dedicated post-quantum security team establi
content:
Industry: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain
Threat: Ethereum Foundation PQC initiative: Dedicated post-quantum security team established January 2026 with $2M in research prizes. Active development of account abstraction and Verkle tree migration paths. All Ethereum accounts that have transacted expose public keys making them quantum-vulnerable via secp256k1 ECDSA and BLS12-381.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: secp256k1 ECDSA, BLS12-381, keccak256 address derivation
PQC Replacement: Account Abstraction (EIP-4337), Verkle Trees, quantum-resistant signature schemes
Source: Ethereum Foundation PQC Program

title: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain — Blockchain HNDL permanence risk: Federal Reserve research confirms distributed l
content:
Industry: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain
Threat: Blockchain HNDL permanence risk: Federal Reserve research confirms distributed ledger networks face permanent data privacy risks from harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks even with future PQC deployment. On-chain transaction data is immutable — encrypted data harvested today remains permanently exposed once CRQCs arrive.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048, ECDSA, ECDH in blockchain protocols, BLS signatures
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87, SLH-DSA, validator PQC authentication
Source: Federal Reserve Board FEDS Paper September 2025

Property deeds, constitutional documents, and notarial acts require 50-100+ year validity. Quantum signature forgery would retroactively undermine the integrity of millions of legally binding documents.

Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048/4096, ECDSA P-256/P-384, SHA-256 in AdES formats (XAdES, PAdES, CAdES)

content:

Wallets must support qualified electronic attestations of attributes. Cryptographic protocols underpinning wallet-to-verifier authentication rely on RSA/ECDSA.

Cryptography at Risk: ECDSA P-256, RSA-2048, ECDH in wallet authentication, X.509 certificates

Timestamps cryptographically prove document existence at specific moments for legal evidence and IP filings. Quantum-capable adversaries could forge timestamps to backdate contracts or fabricate audit trails.

Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048/4096, ECDSA P-256/P-384 in RFC 3161 timestamp tokens, SHA-256

content:

Threat: Court electronic evidence repudiation risk: As quantum computing advances, defense attorneys may challenge the integrity of digitally signed electronic evidence, arguing signatures could have been forged. This introduces reasonable doubt for any evidence authenticated solely with quantum-vulnerable cryptography. Courts will need to establish new standards for digital evidence admissibility in the post-quantum era.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: RSA/ECDSA signatures on court filings, evidence chain of custody, forensic reports
PQC Replacement: PQC re-signing with archival timestamps, SLH-DSA for evidence integrity
Source: NIST SP 800-86 Guide to Integrating Forensic Techniques

content:

title: Rail / Transit — Transit ticketing and access control quantum exposure: ISO/IEC 14443 contactless
content:
Industry: Rail / Transit
Threat: Transit ticketing and access control quantum exposure: ISO/IEC 14443 contactless smart cards and NFC-based ticketing systems used by transit authorities worldwide rely on cryptographic authentication. MIFARE DESFire and similar platforms use AES/3DES symmetric encryption (quantum-resistant) but PKI infrastructure for card issuance, key management systems, and back-office settlement use RSA/ECDSA.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: Ticketing PKI, key management RSA/ECDSA, back-office settlement TLS, mobile ticketing
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM for key management, ML-DSA for PKI, PQC-enabled mobile ticketing
Source: ISO/IEC 14443 Contactless Card Standards

title: Retail / E-Commerce — E-commerce payment flow quantum vulnerability: PCI DSS 4.0.1 requires strong cry
content:
Industry: Retail / E-Commerce
Threat: E-commerce payment flow quantum vulnerability: PCI DSS 4.0.1 requires strong cryptography for online payment processing but TLS protecting checkout flows uses RSA/ECDSA key exchange vulnerable to quantum attacks. E-commerce transaction volume exceeds $6 trillion globally with payment data in transit continuously exposed.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: TLS 1.2/1.3 RSA/ECDSA key exchange, payment tokenization, POS encryption
PQC Replacement: Hybrid TLS 1.3 with ML-KEM, PQC-enabled payment gateways, quantum-safe tokenization
Source: PCI Security Standards Council

title: Retail / E-Commerce — Customer data HNDL exposure: Retailers store customer PII, loyalty program data,
content:
Industry: Retail / E-Commerce
Threat: Customer data HNDL exposure: Retailers store customer PII, loyalty program data, payment history, and behavioral analytics for 5-10+ years for marketing and compliance. NIST Cybersecurity Framework identifies data protection as core function. Encrypted customer databases harvested today become readable with CRQCs.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: Database encryption (RSA/ECDSA key management), CRM system TLS, loyalty platform crypto
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM for database key management, AES-256-GCM, hybrid encryption for customer data
Source: NIST Cybersecurity Framework

title: Retail / E-Commerce — Retail supply chain authentication: GS1 standards govern product identification
content:
Industry: Retail / E-Commerce
Threat: Retail supply chain authentication: GS1 standards govern product identification and supply chain visibility for billions of products globally. RFID, EDI (AS2/AS4), and electronic product codes use digital signatures for authentication. Quantum forgery enables counterfeit product injection and supply chain manipulation.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: EDI AS2/AS4 encryption, RFID authentication, GS1 Digital Link signatures
PQC Replacement: ML-DSA for supply chain signing, PQC-enabled EDI, quantum-safe RFID
Source: GS1 Global Standards

title: Supply Chain / Logistics — Maritime cybersecurity quantum exposure: IMO MSC-FAL.1/Circ.3 guidelines address
content:
Industry: Supply Chain / Logistics
Threat: Maritime cybersecurity quantum exposure: IMO MSC-FAL.1/Circ.3 guidelines address maritime cybersecurity. Maritime sector carries approximately 80% of world trade by volume. Port management systems, AIS (Automatic Identification System), and electronic navigation use RSA/ECDSA for authentication.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: AIS encryption, port management system PKI, electronic navigation authentication
PQC Replacement: ML-DSA for maritime authentication, PQC-enabled AIS, quantum-safe port systems
Source: IMO Maritime Cyber Risk Management Guidelines

title: Supply Chain / Logistics — Electronic Bill of Lading quantum vulnerability: DCSA standards for electronic B
content:
Industry: Supply Chain / Logistics
Threat: Electronic Bill of Lading quantum vulnerability: DCSA standards for electronic Bills of Lading (eBL) enable digital trade documentation for containerized shipping. Digital signatures on eBL documents prove ownership and transfer rights for cargo worth billions. Quantum forgery enables cargo theft and trade document fraud.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: eBL digital signatures (RSA/ECDSA), trade document PKI, shipping platform TLS
PQC Replacement: ML-DSA for eBL signing, hybrid trade document certificates, PQC-enabled shipping platforms
Source: DCSA Electronic Bill of Lading Standards

title: Supply Chain / Logistics — Customs and cross-border trade quantum risk: WCO SAFE Framework of Standards gov
content:
Industry: Supply Chain / Logistics
Threat: Customs and cross-border trade quantum risk: WCO SAFE Framework of Standards governs security and facilitation of international trade. Digital certificates for customs declarations, AEO (Authorized Economic Operator) credentials, and single window systems use RSA/ECDSA. Quantum forgery enables customs fraud at global scale.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: Customs declaration digital signatures, AEO certificates, single window system PKI
PQC Replacement: ML-DSA for customs signing, PQC-enabled single window, quantum-safe AEO credentials
Source: WCO SAFE Framework of Standards

title: Telecommunications — HNDL on subscriber data: Mobile network operators retain call detail records, lo
content:
Industry: Telecommunications
Threat: HNDL on subscriber data: Mobile network operators retain call detail records, location data, and encrypted communications for 2-10 years. 5G user data encrypted with vulnerable algorithms.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: RSA, ECDSA, AES-128 (some legacy)
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA, AES-256
Source: GSMA Security Guidelines

title: Telecommunications — GSMA Post-Quantum Telco Network Taskforce: Established September 2022 to coordin
content:
Industry: Telecommunications
Threat: GSMA Post-Quantum Telco Network Taskforce: Established September 2022 to coordinate quantum-safe migration for mobile network operators worldwide. Published PQ.03 guidelines (July 2025) providing telecom-specific PQC migration guidance. Taskforce brings together major operators and vendors to develop industry-wide PQC migration roadmap for 5G/6G infrastructure.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: 5G authentication (AKA), RAN encryption, IPsec/IKEv2 for network interconnects
PQC Replacement: 3GPP PQC integration, ML-KEM for 5G security, hybrid authentication protocols
Source: GSMA Post-Quantum Telco Network Taskforce

title: Telecommunications — 5G network security quantum vulnerability: 3GPP specifications define 5G securit
content:
Industry: Telecommunications
Threat: 5G network security quantum vulnerability: 3GPP specifications define 5G security architecture (TS 33.501) using ECDSA and RSA for network authentication. 5G network slicing authentication and RAN encryption protect critical enterprise, IoT, and emergency services. N2/N3 interface security relies on IPsec with quantum-vulnerable key exchange.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: 5G AKA protocol, network slice authentication, N2/N3 IPsec/IKEv2, RAN encryption
PQC Replacement: 3GPP Release 19+ PQC integration, ML-KEM for 5G SA, hybrid IKEv2
Source: 3GPP TS 33.501 5G Security Architecture

title: Telecommunications — SIM card and eSIM authentication quantum vulnerability: Billions of SIM cards gl
content:
Industry: Telecommunications
Threat: SIM card and eSIM authentication quantum vulnerability: Billions of SIM cards globally use RSA-2048 or ECDSA P-256 for authentication. GSMA eSIM remote provisioning (SGP.22) relies on PKI with quantum-vulnerable key exchange. SIM replacement cycles of 3-5 years provide a migration window but legacy devices will persist longer.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048, ECDSA P-256 in SIM authentication, eSIM remote provisioning PKI
PQC Replacement: ML-DSA for SIM authentication, PQC-enabled eSIM provisioning, hybrid schemes
Source: GSMA eSIM Specification (SGP.22)

title: Telecommunications — 6G native PQC design window: 6G standards development (2025-2030 in 3GPP and ETS
content:
Industry: Telecommunications
Threat: 6G native PQC design window: 6G standards development (2025-2030 in 3GPP and ETSI) represents a critical opportunity to incorporate PQC from the design phase, avoiding the crypto-agility retrofit challenges facing 5G. ETSI ISG on Quantum-Safe Cryptography is providing input to 6G security architecture development.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: 6G authentication protocols, air interface encryption, network slicing, edge computing
PQC Replacement: Native ML-KEM/ML-DSA in 6G specifications, quantum-safe by design per ETSI QSC
Source: ETSI ISG Quantum-Safe Cryptography / 3GPP

title: Water / Wastewater — Water treatment plant SCADA quantum vulnerability: America's Water Infrastructur
content:
Industry: Water / Wastewater
Threat: Water treatment plant SCADA quantum vulnerability: America's Water Infrastructure Act (AWIA) of 2018 requires community water systems serving 3,300+ people to conduct risk and resilience assessments including cybersecurity. Water treatment SCADA systems use ICS protocols with RSA/ECDSA authentication. CISA identifies Water and Wastewater Systems as one of 16 critical infrastructure sectors. Equipment lifecycles of 15-25 years with limited remote update capability.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: SCADA/ICS authentication (RSA/ECDSA), VPN for remote monitoring, historian database encryption
PQC Replacement: IEC 62443 PQC update for water systems, ML-DSA for SCADA authentication, hybrid VPN
Source: EPA America's Water Infrastructure Act (AWIA) 2018 / CISA

title: Water / Wastewater — Smart water infrastructure quantum exposure: AWWA (American Water Works Associat
content:
Industry: Water / Wastewater
Threat: Smart water infrastructure quantum exposure: AWWA (American Water Works Association) standards govern smart water metering, leak detection sensors, and distribution network monitoring using IoT-connected devices. Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) for water utilities faces similar PQC migration challenges as energy smart meters — constrained devices with limited crypto-agility deployed for 10-15 year cycles.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: AMI mesh networking, smart meter PKI, SCADA remote terminal units, cloud platform TLS
PQC Replacement: Quantum-safe AMI protocols, ML-KEM for device provisioning, constrained device PQC
Source: AWWA Cybersecurity Guidance for Water Utilities
